papers.title: Microstructures, mechanical properties and deformation of near-rapidly solidified low-density Fe-20Mn-9Al-1.2C-xCr steels
papers.paper_scope: The effects of Cr addition on microstructures, mechanical properties and deformation behavior of near-rapidly solidified low-density Fe-20Mn-9Al-1.2C steel strips were investigated.

alloys.alloys_notes: Target nominal composition: Fe-20Mn-9Al-1.2C (wt.%). Measured composition from Table 1.
Target nominal composition: Fe-20Mn-9Al-1.2C-3Cr (wt.%). Measured composition from Table 1.
Target nominal composition: Fe-20Mn-9Al-1.2C-6Cr (wt.%). Measured composition from Table 1.

processes.description: As-cast strips produced by centrifugal casting under near-rapid solidification in argon atmosphere. Cooling rate ~1–1×10^4 K/s. Strip dimensions: 80 mm × 60 mm × 2.5 mm.
processes.processes_notes: Near-rapid solidification refines microstructure, extends solid solubility, and may form metastable phases. This process simulates thin strip continuous casting.

processes.description: Annealing at 600 °C for 3 h in a tubular vacuum furnace applied to as-cast strips.
processes.processes_notes: Annealing promotes precipitation and growth of κ-carbides, increases yield strength.

processes.description: Cold rolling with 20% thickness reduction after stress relief annealing at 400 °C for 1 h. Multipass rolling on a twin roll mill (Φ160).
processes.processes_notes: Stress relief at 400 °C for 1 h was performed before cold rolling. Cold rolling reduces micropores from solidification shrinkage and avoids drastic structural change.

processes.description: Cold rolled with 20% reduction (after stress relief at 400 °C for 1 h) and then further annealed at 600 °C for 3 h.
processes.processes_notes: This state represents CR20% + annealed condition. Annealing after cold rolling promotes κ-carbide precipitation mainly at dislocation entanglements.

structures.overall_structure: Austenite-based duplex microstructure: γ-austenite matrix with irregularly shaped δ-ferrite. Nano-scale κ-carbides in γ-austenite, ordered DO3 phase in δ-ferrite.
structures.microstructure_counts: γ-austenite + δ-ferrite (plus κ and DO3 precipitates).

structures.overall_structure: γ-austenite matrix with increased δ-ferrite volume fraction. Very weak κ-carbide precipitation. DO3 phase in δ-ferrite.
structures.microstructure_counts: γ-austenite + δ-ferrite (plus minor κ and DO3).

precipitates.distribution: very low volume fraction; superlattice reflections barely visible

structures.overall_structure: γ-austenite matrix with further increased δ-ferrite volume fraction. κ-carbides nearly absent. DO3 phase in δ-ferrite.

precipitates.distribution: barely visible; superlattice reflections almost absent

structures.overall_structure: Annealed condition: increased δ-ferrite volume fraction; intense κ-carbide precipitation in γ-austenite; δ-ferrite fully transformed to ordered DO3.
Annealed: δ-ferrite volume fraction 14%; κ-carbides with reduced volume fraction (4%) and size (4 nm); DO3 ordered phase present.
Annealed: δ-ferrite volume fraction 24%; κ-carbides extremely fine and barely visible; DO3 phase present.

size.value: too small to observe in DF

structures.overall_structure: Cold-rolled with 20% reduction; γ-austenite contains {111} planar slip bands; δ-ferrite morphology unchanged, volume fraction 5%.

defects.dislocation_density: Slip bands with average spacing 371 ± 47 nm; high dislocation density on {111} planes

structures.overall_structure: Cold-rolled: slip bands with finer spacing in γ-austenite; δ-ferrite 12% volume fraction.

defects.dislocation_density: Slip bands with average spacing 100 ± 15 nm; high density dislocation structure

structures.overall_structure: Cold-rolled: very fine slip bands in γ-austenite; δ-ferrite 18%.

defects.dislocation_density: Slip bands with average spacing 45 ± 13 nm; extremely high density of planar slip bands

structures.overall_structure: CR20% + annealed: slip bands remain; κ-carbides precipitate mainly at dislocation entanglements; δ-ferrite 5%.

defects.dislocation_density: Slip bands and dislocation entanglements retained

structures.overall_structure: CR20% + annealed: slip bands retained; κ-carbides precipitate at dislocations; δ-ferrite 12%.
CR20% + annealed: slip bands retained; κ-carbides too small to observe; δ-ferrite 18%.

size.value: too small to observe in DF

tensile_properties.stress_strain_curves: Fig. 3(a) engineering, Fig. 3(c) true stress-strain and strain hardening rate

strain_hardening_rate.value: clearly decrease at early stage compared to CR20%

characterization_methods.technique: X-ray diffraction (XRD, Rigaku D/Max-2200, Cu Kα λ=0.154056 nm)
characterization_methods.key_findings: As-cast strips consist of γ-austenite and δ-ferrite with ordered DO3. Double-peak between 43.5°–45.0° attributed to (110)δ and (220)DO3; Cr promotes δ→DO3 transformation. After annealing, double-peak becomes a single DO3 peak.

characterization_methods.purpose: Microstructure observation and δ-ferrite volume fraction measurement
characterization_methods.key_findings: Irregular δ-ferrite distributed in γ matrix. Volume fraction of δ-ferrite increases with Cr: as-cast 3%,12%,18%; annealed 6%,14%,24%; cold-rolled 5%,12%,18%.

characterization_methods.purpose: Characterization of κ-carbides and DO3 ordered phase in as-cast strips
characterization_methods.key_findings: 0Cr: κ-carbides 3% vol, size ~6 nm. DO3 size 26±3 nm. 3Cr: κ very weak; DO3 size 30±3 nm. 6Cr: κ barely visible; DO3 size 28±4 nm. κ-carbide lattice parameter ~0.387–0.389 nm.
Annealing at 600°C promotes κ precipitation. 0Cr: κ 7% vol, size 8 nm. 3Cr: κ 4% vol, size 4 nm. 6Cr: κ extremely fine.

characterization_methods.purpose: Observation of deformation substructure after cold rolling
characterization_methods.key_findings: {111} planar slip bands in γ-austenite. Spacing: 0Cr 371±47 nm, 3Cr 100±15 nm, 6Cr 45±13 nm. Higher slip band density with Cr addition.

characterization_methods.technique: Uniaxial tensile testing (MTS Criterion 44, GB/T-228.1-2010, strain rate 2.5×10⁻⁴ s⁻¹)
characterization_methods.key_findings: YS decreases and UTS×TE increases with Cr in as-cast condition. After cold rolling, Cr-containing steels show higher strength. Strain hardening rate affected by κ-carbide content.

unmapped_findings: The product of ultimate tensile strength and total elongation (UTS×TE) is highest in as-cast 6Cr steel strip (38.0 GPa%).
Cr addition increases both volume fraction and size of δ-ferrite, from 3% to 18% in as-cast and from 6% to 24% in annealed condition.

During tensile deformation of annealed 0Cr and 6Cr strips, dislocation entanglements occur at phase boundaries between ferrite and austenite; slip bands can extend through low-angle grain boundaries (misorientation 2.56°), creating steps.

A double (Slip-band and Microband) hardening mechanism is proposed: slip-band refinement produces the first hump in strain hardening rate, and microband formation produces the second hump at higher strains. The number of nano-scale κ-carbides controls the early-stage strain hardening rate.
Glide plane softening occurs as sheared κ-carbides reduce their pinning effect, making dislocation glide easier and concentrating the effect of κ-carbides at early strain stages.

The decrease of κ-carbides with Cr addition facilitates the formation of high-density slip bands during cold rolling, causing higher strength and strain hardening rate in Cr-containing cold-rolled strips.
The measured average density of the steel strips is 6.71 g/cm³ (0Cr), 6.69 g/cm³ (3Cr), and 6.66 g/cm³ (6Cr).
In CR20%+annealed strips, κ-carbides mainly precipitate at dislocation entanglements; annealing does not eliminate pre-existing slip bands, so yield strength does not significantly increase.
Cr addition expands solid solubility of Al and C in austenite, reducing driving force for κ-carbide formation, consistent with CALPHAD predictions.
